LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICIA MONTANDON'S MOTION TO DISMISS (CCP 583.310; 583.410; 583.420(A)) CONTINUED FROM APR-30-2009 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAY-07-2009, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO MAY 7, 2009 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: ANN MENDOZA, CSR #6812

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICIA MONTANDON'S MOTION TO DISMISS (CCP 583.310; 583.410; 583.450(A)). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 1141.17(B) TOLLED THE FIVE YEAR STATUTE. BOTH PARTIES HAVE DELAYED THE LITIGATION IN THIS CASE. POLICY FAVORING TRIAL OR OTHER DISPOSITION ON THE MERITS IS PREFERRED OVER POLICY THAT REQUIRED DISMISSAL FOR FAILURE TO PROCEED WITH REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN THE PROSECUTION OF AN ACTION. COUNSEL WAIVE NOTICE OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss (Ccp 583.310; 583.410; 583.420(A))
Off Calendar May-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PATRICIA MONTANDON'S Motion to Dismiss (CCP 583.310; 583.410; 583.450(A)). Appearances: Michael Silberstein, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 454-8188 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jerome Sapiro, Jr. The Sapiro Law Firm, (415) 771-0100, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to dismiss is denied. CCP section 1141.17(b) tolled the five year statute. Both parties have delayed the litigation in this case. Policy favoring trial or other disposition on the merits is preferred over policy that required dismissal for failure to proceed with reasonable diligence in the prosecution of an action. Counsel waive notice of the Court's ruling. (302/CWW/xCH)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
